title: Random-walk simulator
acceptance_criteria: src/ingestion/market-data-sim.ts exports class MarketDataSimulator implements BrokerFeed
Constructor takes { clock: Clock, tickIntervalMs?: number (default from SIM_TICK_INTERVAL_MS env var, default 500) }

Simulates a NIFTY spot starting around 22000 with a random walk (±0.05% per tick, normally distributed); derives ATM CE/PE from simulated spot using instrument-registry (or a simplified inline ATM calculation if T-11 not yet available — note dependency)

Actually: T-08 depends on T-07 (interface) and T-04 (Redis) only; it should NOT import from T-11 (instrument-registry) to avoid circular dependency during parallel implementation. Use a simplified ATM calculation inline: atmStrike = Math.round(spot / 50) * 50
connect() starts the tick generation loop using clock.tick(tickIntervalMs, callback) for VirtualClock compatibility; in RealClock mode uses setInterval
Each tick publishes a BrokerTick to the Redis stream STREAM_TICKS via streamPublish (T-04)
Emits 'tick' events for: NIFTY index (isIndex: true), ATM CE (isIndex: false, optionType: 'CE'), ATM PE (isIndex: false, optionType: 'PE'), NSE:INDIAVIX-INDEX (simulated VIX between 12 and 25)
The simulator uses VirtualClock.advance() to advance simulated time — tests can inject VirtualClock and call advance() to produce deterministic tick sequences without wall-clock delays
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
